description: Equisetum arvense, commonly known as field horsetail or common horsetail, is a species of vascular plant in the horsetail family Equisetaceae. Like all horsetails, it is a type of fern. It is native throughout the arctic and temperate regions of the Northern Hemisphere. It has two distinct growth forms, a fertile spore-bearing stem and a sterile green stem. It is sometimes confused with mare's tail, Hippuris vulgaris.
synonyms: ["Equisetum arvense var. nanum", "Equisetum arvense var. robustum", "Equisetum arvense var. serotinum", "Equisetum arvense var. nemorosum", "Equisetum arvense var. irriguum", "Equisetum alpestre", "Equisetum boreale", "Equisetum arvense f. diffusum", "Equisetum arvense var. granulatum", "Equisetum arvense f. pseudonemorosum"]

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: temperate
care.toxicity_note: Toxic to Horses. Signs: Thiamine deficiency: weakness, weight loss, staggers, tremors, death
care.toxicity_symptoms: Thiamine deficiency: weakness, weight loss, staggers, tremors, death
care.used_for: ["It has been used as a remedy for a variety of ailments by Native Americans", "and in ancient Roman", "and Chinese medicine."]
care.used_for_details: It has been used as a remedy for a variety of ailments by Native Americans, and in ancient Roman, and Chinese medicine.
care.edible_parts: The buds are eaten as a vegetable in parts of Asia

description: Equisetum bogotense, the Andean horsetail, is a herbaceous perennial that reproduces through spores. It has thicker, less bushy whorled branches, and a silica rich rhizomatous stem, which roots grow out of, under ground. This stem is a dull dark brown color with glabrous growth aside from the sheathed segments. The plant has a history as a traditional herbal remedy, and a study of its diuretic effects on humans showed significant increases in urinary sodium, potassium, and chloride.
origin: AGW SL, AGS, CLN AN, BZC, AGE CH
synonyms: ["Equisetum bogotense var. polystachyum", "Equisetum bogotense var. nudum", "Equisetum stipulaceum", "Equisetum rinihuense", "Equisetum bogotense var. flagelliforme", "Paramochaete bogotense"]

description: Equisetum fluviatile, the water horsetail or swamp horsetail, is a vascular plant in the horsetail family Equisetaceae. It is a perennial herbaceous pteridophyte that reproduces using spores.
origin: VER OO, MAS, ABT, BLT LA, YUG MA
synonyms: ["Equisetum limosum", "Equisetum maximum", "Equisetum lacustre", "Equisetum fluviatile f. uliginosum", "Equisetum fluviatile var. brachycladon", "Equisetum limosum var. candelabrum", "Equisetum limosum var. polystachium", "Equisetum limosum f. linnaeanum", "Equisetum limosum var. minus", "Equisetum fluviatile var. uliginosum"]

description: Equisetum giganteum is a species of horsetail with the common name southern giant horsetail. It is native to South America and Central America, from central Chile east to Brazil and north to southern Mexico.
origin: BZC MS, NIC, AGS RN, AGW CA, CLN AT
synonyms: ["Equisetum giganteum var. digitaliferum", "Equisetum caracasanum", "Equisetum giganteum var. caracasanum", "Equisetum tarapacanum", "Equisetum philippii", "Equisetum bolivianum", "Equisetum brasiliense var. ramosum", "Equisetum giganteum var. chilense", "Equisetum brasiliense f. ramosum", "Equisetum ramosissimum var. dolosum"]

description: Equisetum hyemale, commonly known as rough horsetail or scouring rush, is an evergreen perennial herbaceous pteridophyte in the horsetail family Equisetaceae native to Eurasia and Greenland. It was formerly widely treated in a broader sense including a subspecies in North America, but this is now treated as a separate species, Equisetum praealtum.
origin: CHQ, YUG MA, BLT LA, CHINA, ALB
synonyms: ["Equisetum hyemale var. javanicum", "Equisetum hyemale var. viride", "Equisetum hyemale var. fuscozonatum", "Equisetum alpinum", "Equisetum hyemale f. paleaceum", "Equisetum hyemale subsp. paleaceum", "Equisetum hyemale f. doellii", "Equisetum hyemale var. capillare", "Equisetum hyemale f. paleaceum", "Equisetum hyemale f. viride"]

care.toxicity_note: Human: toxic. Animal: weak toxic. Parts: Aerial part (or whole plant). (Source: TPPT/Agroscope)

description: Equisetum laevigatum is a species of horsetail in the family Equisetaceae. It is known by the common names smooth horsetail and smooth scouring rush. This plant is native to much of North America except for northern Canada and southern Mexico. It is usually found in moist areas in sandy and gravelly substrates. It may be annual or perennial. It grows narrow green stems sometimes reaching heights exceeding 1.5 meters.
origin: ABT, MXC TL, MXT CA, MXS MI, IOW OO
synonyms: ["Hippochaete laevigata var. polystachya", "Equisetum praealtum var. laevigatum", "Equisetum funstonii var. ramosum", "Hippochaete laevigata var. caespitosa", "Equisetum funstonii f. ramosum", "Hippochaete laevigata var. caespitica", "Equisetum funstonii f. polystachyum", "Hippochaete laevigata var. funstonii", "Equisetum funstonii var. nudum", "Equisetum laevigatum f. variegatoides"]

description:
Equisetum myriochaetum, also known as Mexican giant horsetail, is a species of horsetail that is native to Nicaragua, Costa Rica, Colombia, Venezuela, Ecuador, Peru and Mexico. 
It is the largest horsetail species, commonly reaching 4.6 metres (15 ft), with the largest recorded specimen having a height of 7.3 metres (24 ft). At each node is a whorl of as many as 32 branchlets. It is semi-aquatic and is often found growing on riverbanks.
origin: MEXICO, MXN BC, NIC, MXN BS, MXC TL
synonyms: ["Equisetum mexicanum", "Equisetum mexicanum", "Equisetum ramosissimum var. sprucei", "Hippochaete myriochaeta", "Equisetum elongatum var. muelleri", "Equisetum ramosissimum var. muelleri", "Equisetum myriochaetum var. densum", "Equisetum myriochaetum var. laxum"]

description: Equisetum palustre, the marsh horsetail, is a perennial herbaceous pteridophyte belonging to the subclass of horsetails (Equisetidae). It is widespread in cooler regions of Eurasia and North America.
origin: CHQ, VER OO, MAS, POR OO, ABT

description: Equisetum pratense, commonly known as meadow horsetail, shade horsetail or shady horsetail, is a widespread horsetail (Equisetophyta) and it is a pteridophyte. Shade horsetail can be commonly found in forests with tall trees or very thick foliage that can provide shade and tends to grow closer and thicker around streams, ponds and rivers. The specific epithet pratense is Latin, meaning pasture or meadow dwelling.
origin: ITA VC, KRY OO, RUW OO, EASTERN CANADA, YAK OO
synonyms: ["Equisetum pratense var. ramosissimum", "Equisetum pratense f. serotinum", "Equisetum pratense f. praecox", "Equisetum arvense f. irriguum", "Equisetum pratense f. nanum", "Equisetum ehrhartii", "Equisetum pratense f. sphacelatum", "Equisetum drummondii", "Equisetum umbrosum", "Equisetum pratense var. sphacelatum"]

description: Equisetum sylvaticum, the wood horsetail, is a horsetail native to the Northern Hemisphere, occurring in North America and Eurasia. Because of its lacy appearance, it is considered among the most attractive of the horsetails.
origin: VER OO, MAS, ABT, BLT LA, YUG MA

description: Equisetum telmateia, the great horsetail, is a species of Equisetum (horsetail) native to Europe, western Asia and northwest Africa. It was formerly widely treated in a broader sense including a subspecies in western North America, but this is now treated as a separate species, Equisetum braunii.
